programmes.show_description: Do Me A Favour. Stars: Adam Faith, Iain Cuthbertson & Lynn Dalby. Hazel's cousin Herbert arrives in London and Budgie is bribed into taking him on a guided tour.
1950. True story of a woman imprisoned in several Japanese war camps. She struggles to protect her young son. Stars Claudette Colbert.
1966. Comedy. Directed by Francis A Searle. Stars Barbara Mullen and Eric Woodburn. A spinster has been proclaimed dead - but is very much alive...
1957. Musical. A disc-jockey gets his own TV show and becomes a star. Starring Jackie Collins and featuring many musical acts of the 50's.
1965. Invitation To Limbo. Crime series produced by Aaron Spelling & stars Anne Francis. An electronics firm hires Honey to find an industrial spy who has been leaking company secrets.
Broomstick Wedding. 1956. Stars Scott Forbes & Mike Connors. An angry farmer mistakenly believes that Jim is the man who 'dishonoured' his daughter and has to right his wrong by marrying her.
1943. Romantic Comedy. Directed by George Stevens. Stars Jean Arthur, Joel McCrea & Charles Coburn. During the wartime housing shortage, a woman sublets half her apartment to two bachelors.
1964. Drama. Directed by Frank Nesbitt. Stars Dan Duryea, Isa Miranda, Gwen Watford & Peter Madden. In this great murder mystery, the only clue to the killer's identity is his shoes!
1949. Drama. Directed by George King. Stars Douglass Montgomery, Hazel Court & Patricia Burke. An ex-serviceman plans to escape his faithless wife.
1942. Drama. Director: Lawrence Huntington. Stars Clifford Evans, Patricia Roc, David Farrar, Robert Beatty, William Hartnell. Two bank robbers try to find their partner, who fled to London.
The Secret Death. 1958. Stars: Conrad Phillips, Willoughby Goddard & Jennifer Jayne. When his wife is captured by Gessler's troops and sentenced to death, Tell offers himself in exchange.
1957. Drama. Director Roy Ward Baker. Stars Hardy Kruger, Colin Gordon & Michael Goodliffe. A German flight officer is shot down over England and makes numerous attempts to escape to fight again.
1956. Drama. Director: Pierre Gaspard-Huit. Stars Brigitte Bardot, Micheline Presle & Louis Jourdan. A country girl is discovered by a Paris magazine and becomes a top model.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life It's Odds on the Fish
programmes.show_description: 1968. A look into the art of angling, with a consideration for the problems of ensuring that angling continues as a form of sport and relaxation.
Dutchman's Reef. 1961. Stars Peter Graves, Anthony Wickert & Leonard Teale. Mrs. Culbert summons Cobb to search for her missing son but he is not interested until they come to an agreement.
The Terbuf Affair. 1964. Stars Robert Vaughn, David McCallum & Leo G. Carroll. Solo and Kuryakin are spending some time off in Rome when Solo meets an old flame who is in need of help.
The Nightlifers. Stars Derek Fowlds, John Gregson, Alexander Davion and Daphne Anderson. A group of rich young people go from endless parties aboard a houseboat to violent crimes in the city.
Murders in Collioure - Meurtres à Collioure. 2015. Director: Bruno Garcia. Stars Stéphane Freiss & Héléna Noguerra. A young woman is found murdered echoing a local medieval legend of a witch.
A cursed night is in store for all Cellar Dwellers, as Caroline Munro invites us into the world of female vampires, with The World of Hammer episode Vamp.
Vamp. 1994. Narrated by Oliver Reed. A look at Hammer's depiction of female vampires, which was consistently groundbreaking and controversial, with the fine line trodden between desire and evil.
1977. Director: Richard Loncraine. Stars Mia Farrow, Kier Dullea & Tom Conti. Mourning Julia Lofting moves to London to restart her life, only to be haunted by the ghosts of other children.
Glory of Fulham. Stars Adam Faith, Iain Cuthbertson, Lynn Dalby. Hazel is livid when Budgie arrives home late one night and gets angrier when Budgie tells her that he has a friend waiting outside.
1955. Crime. Director: Joseph H. Lewis. Stars Cornel Wilde & Jean Wallace. A police lieutenant, on a mission to bring down an elusive crime boss, targets the gangster's girlfriend to get her help.
1967. A thoughtful look at the increasing popularity of the countryside and the visitors who spoil it, with a reminder on the need for co-operation in the conservation of the countryside.
Prison Empire. 1961. Stars Stephen McNally, Robert Harland & Harold J. Stone. Investigative reporters Paul Marino & Jack Flood investigate various cases of industrial corruption. (S1, E12)
The Bank Raising. 1964. Stars Buddy Ebsen, Raymond Bailey & Lester Matthews. Invited to the groundbreaking for a new bank, the Clampetts confuse the event with an old-fashioned barn raising.
1949. Drama. Director: Alan Cullimore. Stars Valentine Dyall, Anne Firth, Richard Goolden & Sam Kydd. A dying man hires an assassin to kill so he can frame his death on an enemy.

programmes.show_name: The Night We Dropped a Clanger
programmes.show_description: 1959. A Wing Commander (Brian Rix) is on a mission to find out about the flying bomb. Stars William Hartnell, Leslie Phillips, Leo Franklyn & Liz Fraser.
1952. Comedy. Director: Maurice Elvey. Stars Diana Dors, Thora Hird & John Laurie. A wealthy chairman of Burnville United makes an unethical approach to a star player of another club.
1951. Drama. Directed by Pat Jackson. Stars Googie Withers, James Donald & Petula Clark. An insightful story about the day to day activities within Yeoman's Hospital during the early 1950s.
1952. Crime drama filmed at Denham Studios. Starring Mark Stevens, Jean Kent, John Bentley and Thora Hird. An American test pilot is framed for killing a wartime comrade.
Captain Dan Tempest. 1956. Stars Alec Clunes, Robert Shaw & Peter Hammond. Dan Tempest is arrested for piracy when he returns to New Providence, but he is offered a chance to redeem himself.
The Christmas Party. 1976. Stars Jean Anderson, Patrick O'Connell & Richard Easton. Barbara confesses her troubled marriage to Jennifer. Brian makes Christmas Party plans with an ambivalent Jane.
The Great Gold Robbery. 1958. Stars Robert Beatty, Peter Allenby & John Howlett. Thieves target the Edinburgh to London mail train; the only clue Maguire finds is a footprint by the railway track.

programmes.show_name: The Long & The Short & The Tall
programmes.show_description: 1961. War. Directed by Leslie Norman. Stars Richard Todd, Laurence Harvey & Richard Harris. Based on the Willis Hall play. A British patrol in Burma are determined to avenge British prisoners.
Going Places. A short film showing a beautiful trip across Devon in the 1950s.
1993. Documentary series that acts as a tribute to the greats of British cinema. This episode focuses on a magnificent tribute to Miles Malleson.
Sheep Dogs/Gun Collection. 1981. On a bitterly cold day, Jack Hargreaves travels to Wales for a sheep dog trial. Back in Hampshire he visits a collection of guns from the 'wild west' of America.
1958. Print of Death. Directed by Montgomery Tully. Starring John Warwick, Tim Turner and Phil Brown. A payroll van is pulled over by a police car, the guards shot and the payroll stolen. (S1, E27)
The Thin End. 1980. Stars Bryan Marshall, Clifford Rose & Pamela Salem. Tony lands in an African prison, Monica uncovers damaging information and Janet drops a financial bombshell.
1959. Action. Director: Terence Fisher. Stars Guy Rolfe, Allan Cuthbertson & Andrew Cruickshank. A captain lobbies to investigate the criminal Thugee Cult of Kali, an organised crime group.
1960. Crime noir directed by John Gilling and stars Anthony Quayle, Carl Mohner and Jayne Mansfield, who makes a departure from her usual roles, as tough girl Billy in this dark crime story.
